title: Commercial office, Type V-B, egress corridor below the 44-inch minimum
notes: Tests IBC 1020.3: the narrowest labeled corridor (38 in) is below the 44 in minimum. Declared occupant load is 70 (>= 50), so the 44 in minimum binds and the deterministic rule asserts a hard violation an examiner would cite. Area/stories/exits are clean so only the corridor rule fires.
plan_text:
CODE ANALYSIS A-0.1
Occupancy Group: B
Construction Type: V-B
Stories: 1
Building height: 16 ft
Per-story area: 6,000 sf
Fully sprinklered: yes
Design occupant load: 70
IECC: Title 24 Part 6
Electrical: 200 A main breaker
NFPA 13 sprinkler system. NFPA 72 fire alarm.
Corridor width: 44 in (north). Corridor width: 38 in (south at column). Corridor width: 52 in (lobby).
EXIT 1 (north). EXIT 2 (south). EXIT DOOR 36 in each.
WC 4 LAV 4. CALGreen low-flow fixtures. Construction waste management plan.

ground_truth.rationale: 6,000 sf sprinklered single-story B/V-B is within the Table 506.2 allowance

Narrowest corridor is 38 in, below the 44 in IBC 1020.3 minimum. Declared occupant load is 70 (>= 50), so the 44 in minimum binds and this is a hard violation an examiner would cite (not a needs_review).
